proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant) have shorter mean pfs_months than those with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without visceral metastases.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without bone metastases.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without liver metastases.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.388, p=0.00e+00; strongly negative correlation between ECOG and PFS.
Mean PFS 3.509 mo (mCRPC) vs 4.031 mo (HSPC); diff=-0.521 mo, p=1.58e-181.
Mean PFS 3.706 (visceral=1) vs 3.754; diff=-0.048 mo, p=0.035 (marginally significant).
Pearson r=-0.153, p=3.48e-260; PSA negatively correlated with PFS as hypothesised.
Pearson r=-0.0031, p=0.486; no significant linear association.
Pearson r=0.123, p=1.47e-166; albumin strongly positively associated with PFS.
Pearson r=-0.0167, p=1.81e-04; small but significant negative correlation (much weaker than albumin/PSA).
Pearson r=-0.0102, p=0.023; sign is slightly NEGATIVE (opposite of hypothesis), borderline significant.
Pearson r=-0.0121, p=0.0066; weakly negative, marginally significant.
Mean PFS 3.753 (bone=1) vs 3.742; diff=0.011, p=0.624; not significant.
Mean PFS 3.743 vs 3.744; diff=-0.0017, p=0.948; not significant.

Pearson r=0.856, p=0.00e+00; age is STRONGLY POSITIVELY correlated with PFS in this cohort, opposite of the hypothesised direction. Likely an artefact of how the synthetic outcome was generated; will treat age as a positive prognostic factor going forward.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those not receiving docetaxel (cytotoxic treatment is reserved for sicker patients, confounding-by-indication likely).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving olaparib (driven by BRCA2-positive enrichment).
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving Lu177-PSMA.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.728 (enza+) vs 3.755 (enza-); diff=-0.0263, p=0.153; not significant.
Mean PFS 3.740 vs 3.746; diff=-0.0056, p=0.776; not significant.
Mean PFS 3.732 vs 3.749; diff=-0.0172, p=0.381; not significant.
Mean PFS 3.829 (olap+) vs 3.735 (olap-); diff=+0.094 mo, p=0.0033; positive and significant overall.
Mean PFS 3.741 vs 3.745; diff=-0.0041, p=0.872; not significant.
Mean PFS 3.805 vs 3.741; diff=0.0638, p=0.137; numerically positive but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib (predictive biomarker).
In patients with brca2_mutation=0, treatment_olaparib has little or no effect on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months (effect of olaparib is larger in BRCA2-positive patients).
In patients with psma_high=1, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with longer pfs_months than no Lu177-PSMA.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high on pfs_months.
In patients with msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months.
In patients with ar_v7_positive=1, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to AR-V7-negative patients (AR-V7 confers resistance).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months (resistance).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_abiraterone and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months (resistance).

analyses.result_summary: In BRCA2+ (n=4996): PFS 5.216 (olap+) vs 3.671 (olap-); diff=+1.545 mo, p=3.95e-27. Highly significant benefit.
In BRCA2-: diff=-0.0734 mo, p=0.019; small (slightly negative) effect, marginally significant by sheer N.
Interaction coef = +1.619 mo, p=4.33e-61; very strong positive interaction confirms BRCA2 as a predictive biomarker for olaparib benefit.
Within PSMA-high: diff=-0.0110 mo, p=0.736; not significant. No detectable Lu177 benefit even in PSMA-positive disease.
Interaction coef=-0.0174, p=0.737; no Lu177×PSMA-high interaction detected.
Within MSI-high (n=1528): diff=-0.265 mo, p=0.239; numerically negative, not significant; small sample limits power.
Interaction coef=-0.340, p=0.153; no significant interaction.
Within AR-V7+: diff=-0.0244 mo, p=0.557; no detectable enza effect either way.
Interaction coef=0.0025, p=0.957; no enzalutamide × AR-V7 interaction.
Interaction coef=0.0476, p=0.334; no abiraterone × AR-V7 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months declines monotonically across ECOG performance-status groups (ECOG 0 > 1 > 2).
Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with longer pfs_months than no enzalutamide.
Within mcrpc=0 (HSPC) patients, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with longer pfs_months than no enzalutamide.
Within mcrpc=0 (HSPC) patients, treatment_docetaxel is associated with longer pfs_months than no docetaxel (e.g. CHAARTED-style benefit).
Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with longer pfs_months than no Lu177-PSMA.

analyses.result_summary: PFS by ECOG: 0=4.681 mo, 1=3.492 mo, 2=2.376 mo. Monotonic decrease, ~1.15 mo per ECOG grade.
Within mCRPC: diff=-0.0485 mo, p=0.049; sign is negative, marginally significant — opposite to hypothesis.
Within HSPC: diff=0.0009 mo, p=0.973; not significant.
Within HSPC: diff=-0.0589 mo, p=0.043; sign is NEGATIVE (-0.06 mo), marginally significant — opposite of hypothesis.
Within mCRPC: diff=-0.0102 mo, p=0.764; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Lower bmi is associated with shorter pfs_months (positive correlation between bmi and pfs).
Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.0025, p=0.577; not significant — NLR has no detectable independent association with PFS in this cohort.
Pearson r=-0.0030, p=0.507; not significant.
Pearson r=-0.143, p=9.91e-226; weight loss strongly associated with shorter PFS as hypothesised.
Pearson r=-0.0012, p=0.797; not significant.
Pearson r=0.0020, p=0.656; not significant.
Pearson r=-0.0005, p=0.914; not significant.
Pearson r=-0.0068, p=0.127; not significant.
Pearson r=-0.0065, p=0.144; not significant.
Pearson r=-0.0096, p=0.032; weakly negative, marginally significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without diabetes.
Patients with chronic_kidney_disease=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without CKD.
Patients with heart_failure=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without heart failure.
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity groups (any direction).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type groups (any direction).
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than urban patients.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Patients with depression_anxiety_diagnosis=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: diff=-0.0287 mo, p=0.190; not significant.
diff=0.0334 mo, p=0.231; sign positive (CKD slightly higher PFS), not significant.
diff=-0.0032 mo, p=0.916; not significant.
diff=0.0152 mo, p=0.504; not significant.
r=0.0029, p=0.513; not significant.
r=-0.0037, p=0.402; not significant.
diff=-0.0445 mo, p=0.061; borderline negative, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (heavily pretreated patients fare worse).
Higher years_since_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with prior_chemotherapy=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without prior chemo.
Patients with prior_radiation=1 have different pfs_months from those without prior radiation.
Patients with prior_surgery=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without prior surgery.

analyses.result_summary: r=-0.0057, p=0.205; not significant.
r=-0.0006, p=0.897; not significant.
diff=-0.0097, p=0.592; not significant.
diff=0.0072, p=0.712; not significant.
diff=-0.0299, p=0.116; borderline negative, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without tp53 mutation.
Patients with pten_loss=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without pten loss.
Patients with cdkn2a_loss=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without cdkn2a loss.
At least one of the surveyed pharmacogenomic SNPs (snp_rs* features) shows a nominally significant (p<0.05) association with pfs_months.
Patients with ntrk_fusion=1 have different pfs_months from those without (rare alteration).

analyses.result_summary: diff=0.0031, p=0.866; not significant — TP53 status alone has no detectable PFS effect.
diff=0.0207, p=0.621; not significant on univariate test (becomes significant in adjusted model — see iter 9).
diff=-0.0416, p=0.102; numerically negative, not significant.

Across 25 SNPs, only snp_rs4986893 reached nominal p<0.05 (diff=-0.0921, p=0.015). After Bonferroni correction (25 tests, alpha=0.002) NONE remain significant — consistent with no genuine SNP-PFS association.
diff=-0.1838, p=0.163; numerically lower in fusion-positive but n=247, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS of pfs_months on demographics, disease state, biomarkers, treatments and labs, age_years remains a significant POSITIVE independent predictor of pfs_months (refining h1.12 in adjusted form).
In the same multivariable OLS, ecog_ps remains a significant negative independent predictor of pfs_months.
In the same multivariable OLS, log(psa_ng_ml) remains a significant negative independent predictor of pfs_months.
In the same multivariable OLS, albumin_g_dl remains a significant positive independent predictor of pfs_months.
In the same multivariable OLS, log(ldh_u_l) remains a significant negative independent predictor of pfs_months.
In the same multivariable OLS, weight_loss_pct_6mo remains a significant negative independent predictor of pfs_months.
In the same multivariable OLS, treatment_olaparib remains a significant positive independent predictor of pfs_months (across all patients).

In the same multivariable OLS, brca2_mutation remains a significant positive independent predictor of pfs_months (after adjustment, the BRCA2 main effect is itself protective — most likely a manifestation of the olaparib×BRCA2 mechanism captured separately in iteration 10).
In the same multivariable OLS, psma_high remains a significant positive independent predictor of pfs_months.

In the same multivariable OLS, gleason_score becomes a significant negative independent predictor of pfs_months once other prognostic factors are controlled for (recovers an effect that was hidden in univariate analysis).
In the same multivariable OLS, pten_loss is a significant positive independent predictor of pfs_months (sign opposite to clinical expectation; flagged as a curiosity of the synthetic data).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + bone + liver + log1p(psa) + gleason + albumin + log1p(ldh) + hgb + log1p(alp) + nlr + crp + weight_loss + 6_treatments + brca2 + ar_v7 + msi + psma + tp53 + pten + prior_lines', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS on n=50,000, R²=0.970. Significant predictors (with sign): age_years +0.174/yr (p≈0); ecog_ps -1.151/grade (p≈0); log1p(psa) -0.383 (p≈0); gleason_score -0.0110 (p=7.9e-13); albumin_g_dl +0.477/g/dL (p≈0); log1p(ldh) -0.101 (p=9.3e-112); weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077/% (p≈0); visceral_mets -0.0163 (p=2.9e-05); treatment_olaparib +0.148 (p=4.2e-180); brca2_mutation +0.151 (p=9.6e-185); psma_high +0.025 (p=4.1e-15); pten_loss +0.018 (p=0.010). Non-significant in adjusted model: mcrpc, bone_mets, liver_mets, hemoglobin, log(ALP), nlr, crp, treatment_enzalutamide/abiraterone/docetaxel/lu177_psma/pembrolizumab, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, tp53, prior_lines. Together this confirms h9.1–h9.5, h9.6–h9.11 with the directions stated.
age coef = +0.1738 mo per year, p=0.0e+00.
ecog_ps coef = -1.1510 mo per grade, p=0.0e+00.
albumin coef = +0.4768 mo per g/dL, p=0.0e+00.
weight_loss_pct_6mo coef = -0.0770 mo per %, p=0.0e+00.
treatment_olaparib coef = +0.1479 mo, p=4.2e-180.
brca2_mutation coef = +0.1511 mo, p=9.6e-185.
psma_high coef = +0.0250 mo, p=4.1e-15.
gleason_score adjusted coef = -0.0110, p=7.9e-13 — significant negative effect emerges only after adjustment.
pten_loss adjusted coef = +0.0184 mo, p=0.010 — modest positive effect (counter-clinical, flagged).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, mCRPC, visceral mets, albumin and log(LDH), the positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months remains highly significant (refines h3.3 in covariate-adjusted form).
After covariate adjustment, the interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high on pfs_months is still not significant.
After covariate adjustment, the interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months is still not significant.
After covariate adjustment, treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive interaction on pfs_months is not significant.
There is no positive interaction between treatment_docetaxel and visceral_mets on pfs_months (chemo benefit is not larger in visceral disease).
There is no significant treatment_docetaxel × ecog_ps interaction on pfs_months (the docetaxel effect does not depend on performance status).
There is no significant albumin_g_dl × mcrpc interaction on pfs_months (albumin is prognostic regardless of castration status).
There is no significant nlr × crp_mg_l interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: ols('pfs ~ olap*brca2 + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + albumin + log1p(ldh)')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib×BRCA2 interaction coef = +1.638 mo, p=4.38e-76; effect actually becomes slightly larger after adjustment.
Adjusted Lu177×PSMA-high coef=-0.0397, p=0.396; not significant.
Adjusted pembro×MSI-high coef=-0.230, p=0.284; sign negative, not significant.
Adjusted enza×AR-V7 coef=-0.0062, p=0.882; not significant.
Adjusted docetaxel×visceral coef=0.0336, p=0.449; not significant.
Adjusted docetaxel×ecog coef=0.0086, p=0.743; not significant.
Adjusted albumin×mCRPC coef=0.0126, p=0.702; not significant — albumin is uniformly prognostic.
Adjusted NLR×CRP coef=-0.00066, p=0.117; not significant.
